verses.text: The preparations of the heart are man's, but 'the answer of the tongue, is from the LORD'; a man's ways seem clean to himself, but the LORD weighs the spirits; 'commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established.' God oversees all. Entrust your plans to Him, and they will stand.

The preparations of the heart are man's, but 'the answer of the tongue, is from the LORD'; a man's ways seem clean to himself, but the LORD weighs the spirits; 'commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established.' God oversees all. Entrust your plans to Him, and they will stand.

The preparations of the heart are man's, but 'the answer of the tongue, is from the LORD'; a man's ways seem clean to himself, but the LORD weighs the spirits; 'commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established.' God oversees all. Entrust your plans to Him, and they will stand.

'The LORD hath made all things for himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil'; the proud heart is abomination to Him; 'by mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the LORD men depart from evil.' God rules over all. Reverence for Him turns the heart from sin.

'The LORD hath made all things for himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil'; the proud heart is abomination to Him; 'by mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the LORD men depart from evil.' God rules over all. Reverence for Him turns the heart from sin.

'The LORD hath made all things for himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil'; the proud heart is abomination to Him; 'by mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the LORD men depart from evil.' God rules over all. Reverence for Him turns the heart from sin.

'When a man's ways please the LORD, he maketh even his enemies to be at peace with him'; better a little with righteousness than great revenue without right; 'a man's heart deviseth his way: but the LORD directeth his steps.' God overrules our plans. The life that pleases Him is guided and shielded.

God overrules our plans. The life that pleases Him is guided and shielded.

God overrules our plans. The life that pleases Him is guided and shielded.

A divine sentence is in the king's lips; a just weight is the LORD's; 'it is an abomination to kings to commit wickedness: for the throne is established by righteousness.' Authority is a trust. Rulers stand by justice, and honesty is required of them above all.

Righteous lips delight kings, and they love him that speaks right; a king's wrath is as messengers of death, but the wise pacify it; 'in the light of the king's countenance is life.' Wisdom navigates power. A wise man knows how to turn anger to favour.

'How much better is it to get wisdom than gold!' the highway of the upright is to depart from evil; 'pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.' Wisdom outvalues wealth. And nothing topples a man so surely as his own pride.

'How much better is it to get wisdom than gold!' the highway of the upright is to depart from evil; 'pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.' Wisdom outvalues wealth. And nothing topples a man so surely as his own pride.

Better to be of a humble spirit with the lowly than divide spoil with the proud; he that handles a matter wisely finds good; 'the wise in heart shall be called prudent: and the sweetness of the lips increaseth learning.' Humility is gain. Wise and gracious speech makes teaching welcome.

Better to be of a humble spirit with the lowly than divide spoil with the proud; he that handles a matter wisely finds good; 'the wise in heart shall be called prudent: and the sweetness of the lips increaseth learning.' Humility is gain. Wise and gracious speech makes teaching welcome.

Understanding is a wellspring of life to its owner, but folly is its own punishment; the wise heart teaches the mouth; 'pleasant words are as an honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.' Wisdom flows into speech. Kind words are both sweet and healing.

'There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death'; the labouring man labours for himself; an ungodly man digs up evil, and his lips are like a burning fire. Self-deceit is deadly. The wicked man's mouth scorches like flame.

'There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death'; the labouring man labours for himself; an ungodly man digs up evil, and his lips are like a burning fire. Self-deceit is deadly. The wicked man's mouth scorches like flame.

'There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death'; the labouring man labours for himself; an ungodly man digs up evil, and his lips are like a burning fire. Self-deceit is deadly. The wicked man's mouth scorches like flame.

A froward man sows strife, and a whisperer separates close friends; a violent man entices his neighbour into a way not good; he shuts his eyes to devise schemes. Gossip divides. The mischief-maker plots harm even with his glances and gestures.

'The hoary head is a crown of glory, if it be found in the way of righteousness'; he that is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and he that rules his spirit than he that takes a city; the lot is cast, but its whole disposing is the LORD's. Self-mastery beats conquest. God governs even what seems chance.
